verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचकामस्तु वासुदेवांशो दग्ध: प्राग् रुद्रमन्युना ।देहोपपत्तये भूयस्तमेव प्रत्यपद्यत ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācakāmas tu vāsudevāṁśodagdhaḥ prāg rudra-manyunādehopapattaye bhūyastam eva pratyapadyata
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Kāmadeva [Cupid], an expansion of Vāsudeva, had previously been burned to ashes by Rudra’s anger. Now, to obtain a new body, he merged back into the body of Lord Vāsudeva.

verses.sanskrit: स एव जातो वैदर्भ्यां कृष्णवीर्यसमुद्भ‍व: ।प्रद्युम्न इति विख्यात: सर्वतोऽनवम: पितु: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa eva jāto vaidarbhyāṁkṛṣṇa-vīrya-samudbhavaḥpradyumna iti vikhyātaḥsarvato ’navamaḥ pituḥ
verses.text: He took birth in the womb of Vaidarbhī from the seed of Lord Kṛṣṇa and received the name Pradyumna. In no respect was He inferior to His father.

verses.sanskrit: तं शम्बर: कामरूपी हृत्वा तोकमनिर्दशम् ।स विदित्वात्मन: शत्रुं प्रास्योदन्वत्यगाद् गृहम् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ śambaraḥ kāma-rūpīhṛtvā tokam anirdaśamsa viditvātmanaḥ śatruṁprāsyodanvaty agād gṛham
verses.text: The demon Śambara, who could assume any form he desired, kidnapped the infant before He was even ten days old. Understanding Pradyumna to be his enemy, Śambara threw Him into the sea and then returned home.

verses.sanskrit: तं निर्जगार बलवान् मीन: सोऽप्यपरै: सह ।वृतो जालेन महता गृहीतो मत्स्यजीविभि: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ nirjagāra balavānmīnaḥ so ’py aparaiḥ sahavṛto jālena mahatāgṛhīto matsya-jīvibhiḥ
verses.text: A powerful fish swallowed Pradyumna, and this fish, along with others, was caught in a huge net and seized by fishermen.

verses.sanskrit: तं शम्बराय कैवर्ता उपाजह्रुरुपायनम् ।सूदा महानसं नीत्वावद्यन् सुधितिनाद्भ‍ुतम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ śambarāya kaivartāupājahrur upāyanamsūdā mahānasaṁ nītvā-vadyan sudhitinādbhutam
verses.text: The fishermen presented that extraordinary fish to Śambara, who had his cooks bring it to the kitchen, where they began cutting it up with a butcher knife.

verses.sanskrit: द‍ृष्ट्वा तदुदरे बालं मायावत्यै न्यवेदयन् ।नारदोऽकथयत् सर्वं तस्या: शङ्कितचेतस: ।बालस्य तत्त्वमुत्पत्तिं मत्स्योदरनिवेशनम् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tad-udare bālammāyāvatyai nyavedayannārado ’kathayat sarvaṁtasyāḥ śaṅkita-cetasaḥbālasya tattvam utpattiṁmatsyodara-niveśanam
verses.text: Seeing a male child in the belly of the fish, the cooks gave the infant to Māyāvatī, who was astonished. Nārada Muni then appeared and explained to her everything about the child’s birth and His entering the fish’s abdomen.

verses.sanskrit: सा च कामस्य वै पत्नी रतिर्नाम यशस्विनी ।पत्युर्निर्दग्धदेहस्य देहोत्पत्तिं प्रतीक्षती ॥ ७ ॥निरूपिता शम्बरेण सा सूदौदनसाधने ।कामदेवं शिशुं बुद्ध्वा चक्रे स्‍नेहं तदार्भके ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sā ca kāmasya vai patnīratir nāma yaśasvinīpatyur nirdagdha-dehasyadehotpattim pratīkṣatī
verses.text: Māyāvatī was in fact Cupid’s renowned wife, Rati. While waiting for her husband to obtain a new body — his previous one having been burnt up — she had been assigned by Śambara to prepare vegetables and rice. Māyāvatī understood that this infant was actually Kāmadeva, and thus she began to feel love for Him.

verses.sanskrit: नातिदीर्घेण कालेन स कार्ष्णि रूढयौवन: ।जनयामास नारीणां वीक्षन्तीनां च विभ्रमम् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: nāti-dīrgheṇa kālenasa kārṣṇi rūḍha-yauvanaḥjanayām āsa nārīṇāṁvīkṣantīnāṁ ca vibhramam
verses.text: After a short time, this son of Kṛṣṇa — Pradyumna — attained His full youth. He enchanted all women who gazed upon Him.

verses.sanskrit: सा तं पतिं पद्मदलायतेक्षणंप्रलम्बबाहुं नरलोकसुन्दरम् ।सव्रीडहासोत्तभितभ्रुवेक्षतीप्रीत्योपतस्थे रतिरङ्ग सौरतै: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: sā tam patiṁ padma-dalāyatekṣaṇaṁpralamba-bāhuṁ nara-loka-sundaramsa-vrīḍa-hāsottabhita-bhruvekṣatīprītyopatasthe ratir aṅga saurataiḥ
verses.text: My dear King, with a bashful smile and raised eyebrows, Māyāvatī exhibited various gestures of conjugal attraction as she lovingly approached her husband, whose eyes were broad like the petals of a lotus, whose arms were very long and who was the most beautiful of men.

verses.sanskrit: तामह भगवान् कार्ष्णिर्मातस्ते मतिरन्यथा ।मातृभावमतिक्रम्य वर्तसे कामिनी यथा ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: tām aha bhagavān kārṣṇirmātas te matir anyathāmātṛ-bhāvam atikramyavartase kāminī yathā
verses.text: Lord Pradyumna told her, “O mother, your attitude has changed. You are overstepping the proper feelings of a mother and behaving like a lover.”

verses.sanskrit: रतिरुवाचभवान् नारायणसुत: शम्बरेणहृतो गृहात् ।अहं तेऽधिकृता पत्नी रति: कामो भवान् प्रभो ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: ratir uvācabhavān nārāyaṇa-sutaḥśambareṇa hṛto gṛhātahaṁ te ’dhikṛtā patnīratiḥ kāmo bhavān prabho
verses.text: Rati said: You are the son of Lord Nārāyaṇa and were kidnapped from Your parents’ home by Śambara. I, Rati, am Your legitimate wife, O master, because You are Cupid.

verses.sanskrit: एष त्वानिर्दशं सिन्धावक्षिपच्छम्बरोऽसुर: ।मत्स्योऽग्रसीत्तदुदरादित: प्राप्तो भवान् प्रभो ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: eṣa tvānirdaśaṁ sindhāvakṣipac chambaro ’suraḥmatsyo ’grasīt tad-udarāditaḥ prāpto bhavān prabho
verses.text: That demon, Śambara, threw You into the sea when You were not even ten days old, and a fish swallowed You. Then in this very place we recovered You from the fish’s abdomen, O master.

verses.sanskrit: तमिमं जहि दुर्धर्षं दुर्जयं शत्रुमात्मन: ।मायाशतविदं तं च मायाभिर्मोहनादिभि: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam imaṁ jahi durdharṣaṁdurjayaṁ śatrum ātmanaḥmāyā-śata-vidaṁ taṁ camāyābhir mohanādibhiḥ
verses.text: Now kill this dreadful Śambara, Your formidable enemy. Although he knows hundreds of magic spells, You can defeat him with bewildering magic and other techniques.

verses.sanskrit: परीशोचति ते माता कुररीव गतप्रजा ।पुत्रस्‍नेहाकुला दीना विवत्सा गौरिवातुरा ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: parīśocati te mātākurarīva gata-prajāputra-snehākulā dīnāvivatsā gaur ivāturā
verses.text: Your poor mother, having lost her son, cries for You like a kurarī bird. She is overwhelmed with love for her child, just like a cow that has lost its calf.

verses.sanskrit: प्रभाष्यैवं ददौ विद्यां प्रद्युम्नाय महात्मने ।मायावती महामायां सर्वमायाविनाशिनीम् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: prabhāṣyaivaṁ dadau vidyāṁpradyumnāya mahātmanemāyāvatī mahā-māyāṁsarva-māyā-vināśinīm
verses.text: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] Speaking thus, Māyāvatī gave to the great soul Pradyumna the mystic knowledge called Mahā-māyā, which vanquishes all other deluding spells.

verses.sanskrit: स च शम्बरमभ्येत्य संयुगाय समाह्वयत् ।अविषह्यैस्तमाक्षेपै: क्षिपन् सञ्जनयन् कलिम् ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa ca śambaram abhyetyasaṁyugāya samāhvayataviṣahyais tam ākṣepaiḥkṣipan sañjanayan kalim
verses.text: Pradyumna approached Śambara and called him to battle, hurling intolerable insults at him to foment a conflict.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽधिक्षिप्तो दुर्वाचोभि: पदाहत इवोरग: ।निश्चक्राम गदापाणिरमर्षात्ताम्रलोचन: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’dhikṣipto durvācobhiḥpadāhata ivoragaḥniścakrāma gadā-pāṇiramarṣāt tāmra-locanaḥ
verses.text: Offended by these harsh words, Śambara became as agitated as a kicked snake. He came out, club in hand, his eyes red with rage.

verses.sanskrit: गदामाविध्य तरसा प्रद्युम्नाय महात्मने ।प्रक्षिप्य व्यनदन्नादं वज्रनिष्पेषनिष्ठुरम् ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: gadām āvidhya tarasāpradyumnāya mahātmaneprakṣipya vyanadan nādaṁvajra-niṣpeṣa-niṣṭhuram
verses.text: Śambara whirled his club swiftly about and then hurled it at the wise Pradyumna, producing a sound as sharp as a thunder crack.

verses.sanskrit: तामापतन्तीं भगवान् प्रद्युम्नो गदया गदाम् ।अपास्य शत्रवे क्रुद्ध: प्राहिणोत् स्वगदां नृप ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: tām āpatantīṁ bhagavānpradyumno gadayā gadāmapāsya śatrave kruddhaḥprāhiṇot sva-gadāṁ nṛpa
verses.text: As Śambara’s club came flying toward Him, Lord Pradyumna knocked it away with His own. Then, O King, Pradyumna angrily threw His club at the enemy.

verses.sanskrit: स च मायां समाश्रित्य दैतेयीं मयदर्शितम् ।मुमुचेऽस्‍त्रमयं वर्षं कार्ष्णौ वैहायसोऽसुर: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa ca māyāṁ samāśrityadaiteyīṁ maya-darśitammumuce ’stra-mayaṁ varṣaṁkārṣṇau vaihāyaso ’suraḥ
verses.text: Resorting to the black magic of the Daityas taught to him by Maya Dānava, Śambara suddenly appeared in the sky and released a downpour of weapons upon Kṛṣṇa’s son.

verses.sanskrit: बाध्यमानोऽस्‍त्रवर्षेण रौक्‍मिणेयो महारथ: ।सत्त्वात्मिकां महाविद्यां सर्वमायोपमर्दिनीम् ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: bādhyamāno ’stra-varṣeṇaraukmiṇeyo mahā-rathaḥsattvātmikāṁ mahā-vidyāṁsarva-māyopamardinīm
verses.text: Harassed by this rain of weapons, Lord Raukmiṇeya, the greatly powerful warrior, made use of the mystic science called Mahā-māyā, which was created from the mode of goodness and which could defeat all other mystic power.

verses.sanskrit: ततो गौह्यकगान्धर्वपैशाचोरगराक्षसी: ।प्रायुङ्क्त शतशो दैत्य: कार्ष्णिर्व्यधमयत्स ता: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato gauhyaka-gāndharva-paiśācoraga-rākṣasīḥprāyuṅkta śataśo daityaḥkārṣṇir vyadhamayat sa tāḥ
verses.text: The demon then unleashed hundreds of mystic weapons belonging to the Guhyakas, Gandharvas, Piśācas, Uragas and Rākṣasas, but Lord Kārṣṇi, Pradyumna, struck them all down.

verses.sanskrit: निशातमसिमुद्यम्य सकिरीटं सकुण्डलम् ।शम्बरस्य शिर: कायात् ताम्रश्मश्र्‍वोजसाहरत् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: niśātam asim udyamyasa-kirīṭaṁ sa-kuṇḍalamśambarasya śiraḥ kāyāttāmra-śmaśrv ojasāharat
verses.text: Drawing His sharp-edged sword, Pradyumna forcefully cut off Śambara’s head, complete with red mustache, helmet and earrings.

verses.sanskrit: आकीर्यमाणो दिविजै: स्तुवद्भ‍ि: कुसुमोत्करै: ।भार्ययाम्बरचारिण्या पुरं नीतो विहायसा ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: ākīryamāṇo divi-jaiḥstuvadbhiḥ kusumotkaraiḥbhāryayāmbara-cāriṇyāpuraṁ nīto vihāyasā
verses.text: As the residents of the higher planets showered Pradyumna with flowers and chanted His praises, His wife appeared in the sky and transported Him through the heavens, back to the city of Dvārakā.

verses.sanskrit: अन्त:पुरवरं राजन् ललनाशतसङ्कुलम् ।विवेश पत्न्‍या गगनाद् विद्युतेव बलाहक: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: antaḥ-pura-varaṁ rājanlalanā-śata-saṅkulamviveśa patnyā gaganādvidyuteva balāhakaḥ
verses.text: O King, Lord Pradyumna and His wife resembled a cloud accompanied by lightning as they descended from the sky into the inner quarters of Kṛṣṇa’s most excellent palace, which were crowded with lovely women.

verses.sanskrit: तं द‍ृष्ट्वा जलदश्यामं पीतकौशेयवाससम् ।प्रलम्बबाहुं ताम्राक्षं सुस्मितं रुचिराननम् ॥ २७ ॥स्वलङ्कृतमुखाम्भोजं नीलवक्रालकालिभि: ।कृष्णं मत्वा स्‍त्रियो ह्रीता निलिल्युस्तत्र तत्र ह ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā jalada-śyāmaṁpīta-kauśeya-vāsasampralamba-bāhuṁ tāmrākṣaṁsu-smitaṁ rucirānanam
verses.text: The women of the palace thought He was Lord Kṛṣṇa when they saw His dark-blue complexion the color of a rain cloud, His yellow silk garments, His long arms and red-tinged eyes, His charming lotus face adorned with a pleasing smile, His fine ornaments and His thick, curly blue hair. Thus the women became bashful and hid themselves here and there.

verses.sanskrit: अवधार्य शनैरीषद्वैलक्षण्येन योषित: ।उपजग्मु: प्रमुदिता: सस्‍त्रीरत्नं सुविस्मिता: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: avadhārya śanair īṣadvailakṣaṇyena yoṣitaḥupajagmuḥ pramuditāḥsa-strī ratnaṁ su-vismitāḥ
verses.text: Gradually, from the slight differences between His appearance and Kṛṣṇa’s, the ladies realized He was not the Lord. Delighted and astonished, they approached Pradyumna and His consort, who was a jewel among women.

verses.sanskrit: अथ तत्रासितापाङ्गी वैदर्भी वल्गुभाषिणी ।अस्मरत् स्वसुतं नष्टं स्‍नेहस्‍नुतपयोधरा ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: atha tatrāsitāpāṅgīvaidarbhī valgu-bhāṣiṇīasmarat sva-sutaṁ naṣṭaṁsneha-snuta-payodharā
verses.text: Seeing Pradyumna, sweet-voiced, dark-eyed Rukmiṇī remembered her lost son, and her breasts became moist out of affection.

verses.sanskrit: को न्वयं नरवैदूर्य: कस्य वा कमलेक्षण: ।धृत: कया वा जठरे केयं लब्धा त्वनेन वा ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: ko nv ayam nara-vaidūryaḥkasya vā kamalekṣaṇaḥdhṛtaḥ kayā vā jaṭharekeyaṁ labdhā tv anena vā
verses.text: [Śrīmatī Rukmiṇī-devī said:] Who is this lotus-eyed jewel among men? What man’s son is He, and what woman carried Him in her womb? And who is this woman He has taken as His wife?

verses.sanskrit: मम चाप्यात्मजो नष्टो नीतो य: सूतिकागृहात् ।एतत्तुल्यवयोरूपो यदि जीवति कुत्रचित् ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: mama cāpy ātmajo naṣṭonīto yaḥ sūtikā-gṛhātetat-tulya-vayo-rūpoyadi jīvati kutracit
verses.text: If my lost son, who was kidnapped from the maternity room, were still alive somewhere, He would be of the same age and appearance as this young man.

verses.sanskrit: कथं त्वनेन सम्प्राप्तं सारूप्यं शार्ङ्गधन्वन: ।आकृत्यावयवैर्गत्या स्वरहासावलोकनै: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: kathaṁ tv anena samprāptaṁsārūpyaṁ śārṅga-dhanvanaḥākṛtyāvayavair gatyāsvara-hāsāvalokanaiḥ
verses.text: But how is it that this young man so much resembles my own Lord, Kṛṣṇa, the wielder of Śārṅga, in His bodily form and His limbs, in His gait and the tone of His voice, and in His smiling glance?

verses.sanskrit: स एव वा भवेन्नूनं यो मे गर्भे धृतोऽर्भक: ।अमुष्मिन् प्रीतिरधिका वाम: स्फुरति मे भुज: ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa eva vā bhaven nūnaṁyo me garbhe dhṛto ’rbhakaḥamuṣmin prītir adhikāvāmaḥ sphurati me bhujaḥ
verses.text: Yes, He must be the same child I bore in my womb, since I feel great affection for Him and my left arm is quivering.

verses.sanskrit: एवं मीमांसमानायां वैदर्भ्यां देवकीसुत: ।देवक्यानकदुन्दुभ्यामुत्तम:श्लोक आगमत् ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ mīmāṁsamaṇāyāṁvaidarbhyāṁ devakī-sutaḥdevaky-ānakadundubhyāmuttamaḥ-śloka āgamat
verses.text: As Queen Rukmiṇī conjectured in this way, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the son of Devakī, arrived on the scene with Vasudeva and Devakī.

verses.sanskrit: विज्ञातार्थोऽपि भगवांस्तूष्णीमास जनार्दन: ।नारदोऽकथयत् सर्वं शम्बराहरणादिकम् ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: vijñātārtho ’pi bhagavāṁstūṣṇīm āsa janārdanaḥnārado ’kathayat sarvaṁśambarāharaṇādikam
verses.text: Although Lord Janārdana knew perfectly well what had transpired, He remained silent. The sage Nārada, however, explained everything, beginning with Śambara’s kidnapping of the child.

verses.sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा महदाश्चर्यं कृष्णान्त:पुरयोषित: ।अभ्यनन्दन् बहूनब्दान् नष्टं मृतमिवागतम् ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tac chrutvā mahad āścaryaṁkṛṣṇāntaḥ-pura-yoṣitaḥabhyanandan bahūn abdānnaṣṭaṁ mṛtam ivāgatam
verses.text: When the women of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s palace heard this most amazing account, they joyfully greeted Pradyumna, who had been lost for many years but who had now returned as if from the dead.

verses.sanskrit: देवकी वसुदेवश्च कृष्णरामौ तथा स्‍त्रिय: ।दम्पती तौ परिष्वज्य रुक्‍मिणी च ययुर्मुदम् ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: devakī vasudevaś cakṛṣṇa-rāmau tathā striyaḥdampatī tau pariṣvajyarukmiṇī ca yayur mudam
verses.text: Devakī, Vasudeva, Kṛṣṇa, Balarāma and all the women of the palace, especially Queen Rukmiṇī, embraced the young couple and rejoiced.

verses.sanskrit: नष्टं प्रद्युम्नमायातमाकर्ण्य द्वारकौकस: ।अहो मृत इवायातो बालो दिष्‍ट्येति हाब्रुवन् ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: naṣṭaṁ pradyumnam āyātamākarṇya dvārakaukasaḥaho mṛta ivāyātobālo diṣṭyeti hābruvan
verses.text: Hearing that lost Pradyumna had come home, the residents of Dvārakā declared, “Ah, providence has allowed this child to return as if from death!”

verses.sanskrit: यं वै मुहु: पितृसरूपनिजेशभावा-स्तन्मातरो यदभजन् रहरूढभावा: ।चित्रं न तत् खलु रमास्पदबिम्बबिम्बेकामे स्मरेऽक्षविषये किमुतान्यनार्य: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: yaṁ vai muhuḥ pitṛ-sarūpa-nijeśa-bhāvāstan-mātaro yad abhajan raha-rūḍha-bhāvāḥcitraṁ na tat khalu ramāspada-bimba-bimbekāme smare ’kṣa-viṣaye kim utānya-nāryaḥ
verses.text: It is not astonishing that the palace women, who should have felt maternal affection for Pradyumna, privately felt ecstatic attraction for Him as if He were their own Lord. After all, the son exactly resembled His father. Indeed, Pradyumna was a perfect reflection of the beauty of Lord Kṛṣṇa, the shelter of the goddess of fortune, and appeared before their eyes as Cupid Himself. Since even those on the level of His mother felt conjugal attraction for Him, then what to speak of how other women felt when they saw Him?
